CIPHER.351.cipherString: Spring and autumn are inconsiderable events in a landscape compared with the shadows of a cloud.
CIPHER.351.question: Archaeologists unearth a stone tablet inscribed with a message. It's a quote by Alice Meynell encoded as an Aristocrat. What does it say?
CIPHER.351.author: Alice Meynell

CIPHER.342.question: Your drone photographed some weird markings in a parking lot. Turns out it's a quote in a Cryptarithm. What do the values 9016 657834 2078 decode to?

CIPHER.343.cipherString: One part of wisdom is knowing what you don't need anymore and letting it go.
CIPHER.343.question: A mysterious QR code on the back of a cereal box leads to this encrypted quote by Jane Fonda using the Checkerboard Cipher. The 25th through 30th cipher units (CO TS PA TA OS TT) decode to be WHATYO. What does it say?
CIPHER.343.author: Jane Fonda

CIPHER.330.question: Your chemistry lab's fume hood started beeping in Morse… but the real puzzle is this Cryptarithm quote. What do the values 458 10176 50329 decode to?

CIPHER.332.cipherString: Toil and pleasure, dissimilar in nature, are nevertheless united by a certain natural bond.
CIPHER.332.question: The gym's scoreboard glitched during dodgeball to show this Checkerboard Cipher by Livy with a Polybius key of COMPATIBLE. What does it say?
CIPHER.332.author: Livy

CIPHER.337.cipherString: Obviously the imagination is fueled by emotions beyond the control of the conscious mind.
CIPHER.337.question: Someone left this coded message in the school lab. You are told it's a quote by Joyce Carol Oates encoded with an Aristocrat. You are told that the cipher contains the word THE thrice. What does it say?
CIPHER.337.author: Joyce Carol Oates

CIPHER.348.cipherString: The unending quest of historians for understanding the past -- that is, revisionism -- is what makes history vital and meaningful.
CIPHER.348.question: A time-capsule USB drive from 1983 just auto-opened with this Patristocrat-encrypted message by James M. McPherson using a K2 alphabet. You are told that the cipher starts with THE. What does it say?
CIPHER.348.author: James M. McPherson

CIPHER.345.cipherString: When one begins to search for general solutions, one's doubts about their utility are likely to grow stronger.
CIPHER.345.question: Found taped under a desk in study hall: this Aristocrat puzzle by Democracy and Its Critics using a K1 alphabet. What was the K1 Key Phrase used to encode it?
CIPHER.345.author: Democracy and It's Critics

CIPHER.328.question: You discover a scrap of paper tucked inside an old lab notebook. It contains a quote by Bertrand Russell encoded as a Hill with a keyword of TEMPORARY. What does it say?
CIPHER.328.author: Bertrand Russell

CIPHER.350.cipherString: By attending to the conscious part of ourselves, we contribute to the peace of others as well as ourselves.
CIPHER.350.question: Your lab partner's retainer case clicked open to reveal this Aristocrat-encrypted quote by Warren Farrell. What does it say?
CIPHER.350.author: Warren Farrell

CIPHER.322.question: This coded passage was discovered in the margin of a physics exam from years ago. It's a quote by Heraclitus, encrypted as a Hill Cipher with a keyword of DIRT. What does it say?
CIPHER.322.author: Heraclitus

CIPHER.334.cipherString: The end did justify the means sometimes, otherwise nothing would ever be accomplished.
CIPHER.334.question: You receive this cryptic message from the future through an online time capsule. It's a quote by Steve Perry (author), written in a Complete Columnar. The quote has MEANS somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.334.author: Steve Perry (author)

CIPHER.323.cipherString: El Parlamento mismo no existiría en su forma actual si la gente no hubiera desafiado la ley.
CIPHER.323.question: A quote by Arthur Scargill in Spanish has been encoded using the Xenocrypt Cipher using a K2 alphabet for you to decode. What does it say?
CIPHER.323.author: Arthur Scargill
CIPHER.323.translation: Parliament itself would not exist in its present form had people not defied the law.

CIPHER.339.cipherString: I believe that every person is born with talent.
CIPHER.339.question: A coded message is discovered inside a fortune cookie. It turns out to be a quote by Maya Angelou, encoded using a Baconian Cipher. You are told that the deciphered text starts with IBEL. What does it say?
CIPHER.339.author: Maya Angelou

CIPHER.329.cipherString: You know, as bad as those things are, at least they're predictable. It's the normal people that scare me.
CIPHER.329.question: Scrawled in dry-erase marker on the whiteboard after hours: this Aristocrat encoded quote using a K2 alphabet. What was the K2 Key Phrase used to encode it?

CIPHER.336.cipherString: May all eventually settle in the valley of wisdom.
CIPHER.336.question: While analyzing the background noise from a physics experiment, you detect this pattern. It's a quote by Richard Rensberry, encoded using a Baconian. What does it say?
CIPHER.336.author: Richard Rensberry

CIPHER.324.cipherString: A single conversation with a wise man is better than ten years of study.
CIPHER.324.question: Hidden inside a fortune cookie was a Chinese proverb encoded using a Nihilist Substitution cipher with a keyword length of 5. The 8th through 17th cipher units (44 56 75 96 56 57 65 47 90 45) decode to be CONVERSATI. What does it say?
CIPHER.324.author: Chinese proverb

CIPHER.331.cipherString: We delight in the beauty of butterfly, rarely admit the changes it has gone through to achieve that beauty
CIPHER.331.question: You receive this puzzling text during a live science broadcast. It contains a quote by Maya Angelou, encrypted as an Aristocrat using a K1 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.331.author: Maya Angelou

CIPHER.325.cipherString: How true it is when they say there is nothing which makes a man more furious than the discovery that he has deceived himself!
CIPHER.325.question: Your Bunsen burner's flame suddenly spelled this Patristocrat message by Michael Moorcock using a K1 alphabet. You are told that the cipher contains HOW. What does it say?
CIPHER.325.author: Michael Moorcock

CIPHER.335.cipherString: The best philosophy is to live in the present moment while planning for the future and learning from the past.
CIPHER.335.question: A message flashes briefly on a malfunctioning computer screen. It turns out to be a quote by Shree Shambav written in an Aristocrat. You are told that the cipher contains the word THE four times. What does it say?
CIPHER.335.author: Shree Shambav

CIPHER.346.cipherString: I'm always making a comeback but nobody ever tells me where I've been.
CIPHER.346.question: Someone taped a piece of paper with this sequence onto the back of a trophy from last year's Science Olympiad. It's a quote by Billie Holiday, written using a Nihilist Substitution Cipher with a keyword of GREATS and Polybius key of LONGEVITY. What does it say?
CIPHER.346.author: Billie Holiday

CIPHER.349.cipherString: The distance is nothing; it is only the first step that is difficult.
CIPHER.349.question: Your science fair project logs contain a mysterious string of letters. It's a quote by Madame du Deffand, encoded as a Porta using a keyword of LOAFING. What does it say?
CIPHER.349.author: Madame du Deffand

CIPHER.327.cipherString: The systems approach goes on to discovering that every world-view is terribly restricted.
CIPHER.327.question: Your microscope slide prep strangely revealed this Aristocrat message. What does it say?

CIPHER.341.cipherString: I do like to shock and surprise people. When it's all in good fun, of course.
CIPHER.341.question: A glitchy hologram in the school planetarium displays this Porta version of Ruth Warrick's words. The 32nd through 37th cipher characters (CVWLUD) decode to be WHENIT. What does it say?
CIPHER.341.author: Ruth Warrick

CIPHER.340.cipherString: Can science ever be immune from experiments conceived out of prejudices and stereotypes, conscious or not?
CIPHER.340.question: A message was left on the whiteboard after science club. It's a quote encoded using an Aristocrat using a K2 alphabet. What does it say?

CIPHER.326.cipherString: Business is the art of making money by selling things or services people want for more than their cost.
CIPHER.326.question: You intercepted this transmission from a rogue weather balloon. Decode the Complete Columnar version of Patrick Dixon's famous words. The quote has MONE somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.326.author: Patrick Dixon

CIPHER.344.cipherString: Sometimes small things can become very large, indeed.
CIPHER.344.question: Your robot vacuum just projected this message onto the ceiling. It's a quote by Rick Riordan encoded with a Nihilist Substitution with a keyword of RULING and Polybius key of RESTOCKING. What does it say?
CIPHER.344.author: Rick Riordan

CIPHER.338.cipherString: Be unique. Tread smart. Stay original. Keep being YOU. Don't allow anyone tell you otherwise.
CIPHER.338.question: A coded message appears after you shine a UV light on a classroom poster. It turns out to be a quote by P.C. Clotter, encrypted using a Checkerboard with a Polybius key of EUPHORIC. What does it say?
CIPHER.338.author: P.C. Clotter

CIPHER.347.cipherString: I have a new philosophy. I'm only going to dread one day at a time.
CIPHER.347.question: Found on a microchip inside a disassembled robotics kit: this Baconian Cipher by Charles M. Schulz. What does it say?
CIPHER.347.author: Charles M. Schulz

CIPHER.333.cipherString: Si tienes una debilidad especial, no la expongas tratando de hacer cosas que la saquen a la luz.
CIPHER.333.question: A mysterious postcard arrives from an unknown sender. It contains a coded quote by Spurgeon in Spanish, written in a Xenocrypt using a K1 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.333.author: Spurgeon
CIPHER.333.translation: If you have a special weakness, do not expose it by attempting to do things which will bring it out.
